evidence_rea: You have the right to request that we restrict the processing of your personal data.
evidence_reg: The data subject shall have the right to obtain from the controller restriction of processing where one of the following applies:
per_reg_comparisons.reasoning_short: The REA states a right to request restriction of processing, which is broadly aligned with the REG right to obtain restriction from the controller, but the REG adds a condition ('where one of the following applies') that is omitted in the REA; omission alone is not enough to establish a meaning-changing mismatch.

evidence_rea: You have the right to request that we restrict the processing of your personal data.
evidence_reg: The data subject shall have the right to obtain from the controller restriction of processing where one of the following applies: (b) the processing is unlawful and the data subject opposes the erasure of the personal data and requests the restriction of their use instead;
per_reg_comparisons.reasoning_short: The REA gives a general right to request restriction, while the REG specifies one particular condition under which that right applies; the REA does not directly contradict this condition, and omission alone is not a deviation.

evidence_rea: You have the right to request that we restrict the processing of your personal data.
evidence_reg: The data subject shall have the right to obtain from the controller restriction of processing where one of the following applies: (a) the accuracy of the personal data is contested by the data subject, for a period enabling the controller to verify the accuracy of the personal data;
per_reg_comparisons.reasoning_short: The REA generally states a right to request restriction of processing, and the REG specifies a particular triggering condition and period for one case; there is no direct conflict in the quoted text, only missing detail.
